mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in <55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in real sales of goods and services and housing starts reflect synchronized consumer and business demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals disinflationary pressure that prompts expectations of central bank easing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Disinflationary CPI prints are followed by negative 5d returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate, where payrolls rise but the unemployment rate simultaneously increases, indicates labor market slack that suppresses equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence yields positive 20d returns in >60% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product above 2.0% reflects sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60d returns in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the index of industrial production above its 6-month trend signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production above trend yields negative 5d returns in >55% of cases.
